summary: IAM user svc-scanner performed 14 read-only reconnaissance API calls (DescribeInstances, DescribeSecurityGroups, GetCallerIdentity) via confirmed Tor exit node 198.51.100.77, deviating from its normal baseline source IP (192.0.2.140 at ~02:00 UTC on the two preceding days). The principal name strongly suggests an authorised scanner or pentest service account, making the activity plausibly legitimate, but a compromised credential used for pre-attack reconnaissance cannot be ruled out. A human must confirm whether a pentest or red-team exercise was authorised for this account on 2026-07-13 before any containment action is taken.

evidence.observation: svc-scanner called DescribeInstances x9, DescribeSecurityGroups x4, GetCallerIdentity x1 (14 total) from Tor IP 198.51.100.77 (ASN 64499 / RelayNet, Frankfurt) between 07:52–08:05 UTC on 2026-07-13.
198.51.100.77 is a confirmed, currently active Tor exit node (reputation score 55, tagged tor/anonymiser, 12 abuse reports in 90 days, last seen 2026-07-13).

On 2026-07-11 and 2026-07-12 svc-scanner called DescribeInstances from 192.0.2.140 at ~02:00 UTC — a stable automated baseline. On 2026-07-13 the source shifted to the Tor IP and included a ListBuckets attempt (AccessDenied), a one-off anomaly against the baseline.
